stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 5 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 15 observations with mean 62.2 points (within-school SD = 6.1). school B has 5 observations with mean 88.1 points (within-school SD = 7.2). school C has 5 observations with mean 74.2 points (within-school SD = 5.4). school D has 15 observations with mean 63.8 points (within-school SD = 7.7). school E has 8 observations with mean 90.7 points (within-school SD = 7.7). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.9 and 14.9 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school D: 64.1 points (shrunk from raw 63.8). Range [63.9, 64.6] as between-school SD varies from 5.9 to 14.9. Pooled mean: 71.4.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 71.4 points (weighted average of 5 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=9.9: B = 0.961.
Interval [63.9, 64.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (63.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (71.4) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
